evidence_version: IEEE Transactions on Computational Intelligence and AI in Games 3(3) (2011)
screening_locator: OpenAlex work record, primary-location metadata; IEEE Xplore landing-record request returned HTTP 202
exclusion_reason: The DOI and scholarly-index records identify the report, but no inspectable full text or abstract material was retrievable to establish its track representation, generation operation, or validation evidence.
notes: Exhaustive retrieval attempted through the DOI, IEEE Xplore record, CiteSeerX record, and OpenAlex; CiteSeerX TLS validation failed and IEEE returned an asynchronous access response.

evidence_version: International Journal on Electrical Engineering and Informatics 8(4) (2016)
notes: Transfer mapping: the source's segment-sequence representation and search-generated video-game circuits map directly to a robot-racing centerline and corridor.

screening_locator: Official UNECE landing-page main document record; requested R157r1e.pdf endpoint returned HTTP 403
exclusion_reason: The official record identifies an automated-lane-keeping regulation, but its full text was inaccessible and no inspected material establishes a course-generation, course-representation, or transferable-course contribution.
notes: Exhaustive official-source retrieval attempted at the supplied landing record and three UNECE regulation-PDF locations; all full-text PDF requests returned HTTP 403.
Transfer mapping: generated closed centerlines and inflated road bands map directly to ground-robot racing-course geometry; the documented gate-sequence generator maps to aerial-racing gates.

Transfer mapping: generated lane topology, lane-centerline control points, and boundaries are an adjacent road-course representation that maps to robot course networks without changing the model's generation claim.

Transfer mapping: the source-native script samples buoy coordinates and serializes a RobotX obstacle-course model; the buoy sequence maps directly to a marine robot racing course.
